news.headline: Premier, Inc. (NASDAQ: PINC) declares a $0.21 quarterly cash dividend for shareholders; payable on Dec 15, 2024.
news.summary: Premier, Inc., a prominent healthcare improvement company traded on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol PINC, disclosed that its Board of Directors has approved a cash dividend of $0.21 for each share of Class A common stock. Shareholders owning the stock as of December 1, 2024, will receive the dividend on December 15, 2024. Premier, Inc. focuses on incorporating technology to drive advancements in healthcare and is recognized as a key player in the industry.

news.headline: Fair Isaac Corporation (FICO) to report Q4 fiscal 2024 results on Nov 6, 2024. Conference call at 5:00 pm EST. Webcast available.
news.summary: A leading global analytics software company, FICO, is set to disclose its financial results for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 on November 6, 2024, following the market's closure. A conference call is scheduled for the same day at 5:00 p.m. Eastern time, with alternate starting times of 4:00 p.m. Central and 2:00 p.m. Pacific. Interested parties can tune into the call via webcast on FICO's website at www.fico.com/investors. For those unable to join the live broadcast, a replay will be available on the Event Calendar section under Past Events until November 6, 2025.

news.headline: Federal and state collaboration in Colonial Beach, VA will boost economic development in rural Virginia, led by USDA.
news.summary: Today in Colonial Beach, Virginia, Perry Hickman, the Virginia State Director of the USDA Rural Development, revealed the continuation of a collaborative project that aims to enhance market opportunities. This initiative signifies a significant step in a long-term partnership, promising to generate increased market options.

news.headline: Precision BioSciences presents ARCUS preclinical data at ESGCT Congress; showcasing high-efficiency gene editing. Nasdaq: DTIL.
news.summary: In Durham, North Carolina, Precision BioSciences, Inc. (Nasdaq: DTIL), a company specializing in gene editing, disclosed that they will present a poster at the European Society of Gene & Cell Therapy Congress from October 22-25, 2024, in Rome, Italy. The poster will showcase preclinical findings exhibiting ARCUS' capability to accomplish efficient gene edits. Precision BioSciences is known for utilizing its ARCUS platform to progress in vivo gene editing therapies for complex gene modifications.

news.headline: Info-Tech Research Group releases blueprint to streamline IT service desks, offering strategies to improve efficiency.
news.summary: A leading IT research and advisory company, Info-Tech Research Group, has introduced a detailed plan to streamline IT service desks worldwide. The initiative provides IT executives with practical approaches to address issues such as obsolete technologies, operational inefficiencies, and service inconsistencies. This blueprint is designed to enhance IT service desk operations and ensure greater efficiency.

news.headline: Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) to release Q3 2024 earnings on Nov 7th after market close, with conference call at 4pm Central.
news.summary: Chicago-based technology company, Motorola Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: MSI), is set to report its third-quarter 2024 earnings after the market closes on November 7. The company will conduct a conference call with financial analysts at 4 p.m. Central Time (5 p.m. Eastern Time) on the same day. The conference call will be live-streamed on www.motorolasolutions.com/investors. Motorola Solutions specializes in building and integrating technologies aimed at enhancing safety and security.

news.headline: Energy Transfer LP will pay $0.2111 per Series I Preferred Unit on Nov 14, 2024 to unitholders of record by Nov 4, 2024.
news.summary: In Dallas, Energy Transfer LP (ET) revealed a quarterly cash distribution of $0.2111 for each Series I Preferred Unit (NYSE: ETprI). The said distribution will be paid on November 14, 2024, to Series I unitholders recorded as of November 4, 2024. Energy Transfer LP (NYSE: ET) possesses and manages a vast collection of energy assets across the United States, comprising over 130,000 miles of pipeline infrastructure.

news.headline: Newpark Resources in Texas schedules Q3 2024 results conference call and webcast.
news.summary: Newpark Resources has disclosed that they will be holding a conference call and webcast to discuss their third quarter results for the year 2024. The event is scheduled to take place in The Woodlands, Texas. The company has not specified any precise figures for this announcement.

news.headline: World Kinect Corporation (WKC) reports Q3 2024 results: Volume down 2% to 4,443m, Revenue down to $10,491m from $12,245m in 2023.
news.summary: In Miami, World Kinect Corporation (NYSE: WKC) released its third quarter 2024 financial results. The unaudited report showed a decrease in volume from 4,536 million to 4,443 million, marking a 2% drop compared to the same period in 2023. As for revenue, there was a substantial decrease from $12,245 million in 2023 to $10,491 million in 2024. For the nine months ending September 30, 2024, the volume declined from 13,473 million to 13,230 million, a 2% decrease from the previous year.

news.headline: Universal Logistics Holdings reported Q3 2024 revenue of $426.8M, operating income of $42.6M, EPS of $1.01, and declared a dividend of $0.105 per share. CEO Tim Phillips commented on the company's performance and strategic initiatives. Intermodal segment revenues decreased, while the contract logistics segment saw a significant increase. Trucking segment showed a decrease in revenue. The company's cash and marketable securities were $11.8M and $11.7M respectively, with outstanding debt of $561.2M. Universal held a conference call to discuss the financial results. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: Universal Logistics Holdings, Inc. reported an increase in operating revenue to $426.8 million for the third quarter of 2024, up by 1.3% from the previous year. Operating income also rose to $42.6 million, marking a 16.0% increase, while earnings per share grew by 14.7% to $1.01 per share. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.105 per share. Universal's contract logistics segment saw a significant increase in operating revenue to $245.2 million, a rise of 17.8%, with operating income reaching $45.6 million. However, the intermodal segment experienced a revenue decline of 11.8%, reporting $77.6 million in operating revenue and an operating loss of $(1.1) million. The trucking segment also faced a 10.3% decrease in operating revenue at $87.0 million but achieved an operating income of $7.1 million. Universal highlighted challenges in the freight environment and the closure of its company-managed brokerage business. The company's CEO, Tim Phillips, remains optimistic about growth opportunities and strategic decisions.

news.headline: Financial details for Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. Q3 2024: Revenues $2.77B, Net Earnings $314.1M. CEO optimistic about future growth opportunities.
news.summary: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. reported its financial results for the quarter ended September 30, 2024. Total Company revenues were $2,766.5 million, up from $2,453.6 million in the same quarter last year. Net Earnings were $314.1 million compared to $283.2 million in the previous year. EBITDAC was $690.9 million, up from $606.1 million. Adjusted Earnings Per Share were $1.39, an increase from $1.28. The company reported positive growth in its brokerage and risk management segments. Total revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2024, were $8,720.6 million, up from $7,533.4 million in the prior year, with adjusted Earnings Per Share of $5.40. The company highlighted growth in organic revenues and completed several acquisitions during this period. Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. expressed optimism for future prospects despite challenges such as recent storms and floods impacting clients and employees. CEO J. Patrick Gallagher, Jr. emphasized strong financial performance, revenue growth, and a robust M&A pipeline. The company looks forward to continued success in the global market.

news.headline: Capri Holdings Limited (NYSE: CPRI) to report Q2 fiscal 2025 results on Nov 7, 2024, no conference calls due to pending acquisition by Tapestry, Inc.
news.summary: Capri Holdings Limited (NYSE: CPRI) has revealed that it will unveil its financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025 on Thursday, November 7, 2024, after the market closes through a press release. Due to the anticipated acquisition by Tapestry, Inc., the company does not plan to conduct conference calls to discuss the financial outcomes. Capri Holdings Limited is an esteemed global fashion luxury group led by its founder, with shares traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker CPRI.

news.headline: Shore Bancshares reports $11.2M net income for Q3 2024, $30.6M for 9 months. Total assets at $5.9B, ROAA 0.77%, NIM 3.17%. Total deposits $5.23B, $43.3M net interest income. Non-GAAP ROAA 0.90% for Q3. Allowance for credit losses stable at 1.24%. Operating leverage stable at 67.49% efficiency ratio. Deposits increased to $5.23B. Net charge-offs were $1.4M. Nonperforming assets were $15.8M, stable. Total income: $41.8M after provision for credit losses $1.47M. The Company had total liabilities of $5.38B and total equity of $533.2M.SUMMARY: Shore Bancshares reports stable financials, with $11.2M net income in Q3 2024 and $30.6M for 9 months, total assets at $5.9B, and improvements in ROAA and NIM.
news.summary: Shore Bancshares, Inc., the holding company for Shore United Bank, reported a net income of $11.2 million or $0.34 per diluted common share for the third quarter of 2024. This was equal to the second quarter of 2024. Net income for the first nine months of 2024 was $30.6 million, compared to $0.7 million for the first nine months of 2023. Total assets were $5.9 billion at September 30, 2024, with total deposits at $5.23 billion. Loan growth, increased net interest income, and margin were reported. The Company's tangible common equity ratio at September 30, 2024, was 7.39%. Total noninterest income for the third quarter of 2024 decreased to $7.3 million, while total noninterest expense was $34.1 million. The Company reported an increase in net interest income and net interest margin. Nonperforming assets were at $15.8 million for the third quarter of 2024. The Company's non-GAAP efficiency ratio was 62.10% for the third quarter of 2024. Total stockholders' equity increased to $22.1 million.

news.headline: UHS reports Q3 financial results: net income $258.7M (↑$91.7M from Q3 2023), revenues $3.96B (↑11.2%), and EBITDA $528.6M. Repurchased $153.9M worth of stock in Q3 2024. Full PR on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: Universal Health Services, Inc. reported its third-quarter and nine-month financial results for 2024, with notable increases in net income, revenue, and adjusted EBITDA. In Q3 2024, net income was $258.7 million, up from $167.0 million in Q3 2023, with net revenues increasing by 11.2% to $3.963 billion. Adjusted net income was $252.5 million. For the nine-month period, net income was $809.7 million in 2024, up from $501.4 million in 2023, with net revenues of $11.714 billion. Adjusted net income was $798.2 million. The company repurchased shares and recently completed financing transactions enhancing liquidity. Acute care services and behavioral health care services also reported revenue increases. A detailed breakdown of financial figures, operational performance, and future outlook was provided. For more information, visit the full article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Federated Hermes, Inc. records $800.5 billion in assets, $1.06 Q3 2024 earnings per share, announces $0.31 dividend. Assets hit record $593.0 billion in money market assets and $100.2 billion in fixed income assets. Earnings call scheduled for Oct 25, 2024.
news.summary: Federated Hermes, Inc. reported Q3 2024 earnings per diluted share of $1.06, up from $0.86 for the same quarter in 2023. The total assets under management reached a record $800.5 billion, with money market assets at $593.0 billion and fixed income assets at $100.2 billion. The company's equity assets were $83.6 billion, fixed-income assets were $100.2 billion, and alternative/private markets assets stood at $20.7 billion. The company declared a dividend of $0.31 per share and authorized a new share repurchase program. During Q3 2024, they purchased 818,164 shares of stock for $26.9 million. Revenue for the quarter increased by 1% compared to Q3 2023, primarily due to higher average money market assets. Operating expenses decreased by 1%, while nonoperating income increased. For the YTD 2024, revenue decreased by 1% compared to YTD 2023, attributed to a decrease in carried interest. Operating expenses increased by 2%, primarily due to increased intangible asset expenses. Nonoperating income increased primarily due to the market value of investments in 2024 compared to 2023. The company will host an earnings conference call on Oct. 25, 2024, at 9 a.m. Eastern Time. Federated Hermes is a global leader in responsible investing with assets under management of $800.5 billion.

news.headline: First Financial Bancorp reports Q3 2024 earnings: $0.55 EPS, ROA 1.17%, NIM 4.08%, noninterest income $45.7 million.
news.summary: Adjusted earnings per diluted share reached $0.67, while on a regular basis it was $0.55. Return on average assets stood at 1.42% adjusted and 1.17% ordinarily. The net interest margin on an FTE basis was at 4.08%. Noninterest income totaled $58.8 million adjusted, and $45.7 million originally. There was an average deposit growth of $166.2 million.

news.headline: Carlisle Companies reports Q3 2024 results - Record EPS of $5.30, adj. EPS of $5.78 (+24% YoY), Revenue $1.3B (+6% YoY), Operating margin 23.7%, CCM revenue up 9%. Resi. market headwinds impact CWT.
news.summary: Carlisle Companies Incorporated (NYSE: CSL) reported its financial results for the third quarter of 2024 in Scottsdale, Arizona. The company achieved a record third quarter diluted EPS of $5.30 and adjusted EPS of $5.78, marking a 24% increase year-over-year. Revenue for the quarter reached $1.3 billion, up 6% compared to the previous year. The operating margin stood at 23.7%, with a Q3 record adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.6%, representing a 60 basis point increase year-over-year. Carlisle Construction Materials (CCM) experienced a 9% revenue growth and expanded its adjusted EBITDA margin by 110 basis points to a Q3 record of 32.8%. However, the company faced challenges in residential markets affecting Carlisle WorkTech (CWT).

news.headline: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE: FIX) reports Q3 2024 results: Net income $146.2M, revenue $1.81B, operating cash flow $302.2M.
news.summary: Comfort Systems USA, Inc. announced its quarterly results for the period ending on September 30, 2024. The net income for this quarter was $146.2 million, translating to $4.09 per diluted share. This is an increase from the previous year's $105.1 million, or $2.93 per diluted share for the same period. The revenue for the third quarter of 2024 saw a significant rise to $1.81 billion, compared to $1.38 billion in 2023. Additionally, the Company reported operating cash flow of $302.2 million during this quarter.

news.headline: Everseen promotes AI technology to combat retail theft on Fight Retail Crime Day, highlighting strategies used by shoplifters. Retail crime costs billions annually. Organized retail crime and opportunistic theft pose growing challenges for retailers.
news.summary: In Cork, Ireland and Miami, Everseen, a prominent vision AI company, is combatting theft through advanced AI technology. In honor of Fight Retail Crime Day, the company sheds light on the unconventional tactics employed by shoplifters. Despite the whimsical names associated with these methods, their effect on retailers is substantial, contributing to billions in losses each year. The retail industry is under increasing strain due to rising instances of organized retail crime and opportunistic theft, posing significant risks to their profitability.

news.headline: Chadwick-BaRoss showcases Ponsse Scorpion Giant at demo event in Maine, attended by logging contractors and operators from New England.

news.headline: Business First Bancshares, Inc. announced Q3 2024 results with net income of $16.5 million and core net income of $17.2 million.
news.summary: Business First Bancshares, Inc. (NASDAQ: BFST) based in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, reported its unaudited financial results for the quarter ended September 30, 2024. The company, which owns b1BANK, disclosed a net income available to common shareholders of $16.5 million, translating to $0.65 per diluted common share. These figures represent an increase of $0.6 million and $0.03, respectively, compared to the prior quarter ended June 30, 2024. Excluding specific income and expenses, the core net income for the same quarter was $17.2 million, equivalent to $0.68 per diluted common share. This marks a $1.0 million and $0.04 increase from the linked quarter earlier.

news.headline: Barrick Gold denies Mali govt's claims of breaching commitments for fairer mineral exploitation, Oct. 24, 2024.

news.headline: Mohawk Industries Q3 2024 report: net sales $2.7B, net earnings $162M, EPS $2.55. Adjusted net earnings $184M, adjusted EPS $2.90. Prior-year net sales $2.8B, net loss $760M, loss per share $11.94. Earnings impacted by non-cash impairment charges in Q3 2023.
news.summary: In Calhoun, Georgia on October 24, 2024, Mohawk Industries, Inc. (NYSE: MHK) released their third quarter financial results for 2024. The company reported net earnings of $162 million and earnings per share of $2.55. Adjusted net earnings were $184 million, with adjusted EPS at $2.90. Net sales for the third quarter of 2024 totaled $2.7 billion, showing a 1.7% decrease as reported and a 2.1% decrease on an adjusted basis compared to the previous year. In the third quarter of 2023, Mohawk Industries reported net sales of $2.8 billion, with a net loss of $760 million and a loss per share of $11.94. Adjusted net earnings for the same period were $174 million, with an adjusted EPS of $2.72. The substantial loss in the prior year was mainly due to non-cash impairment charges amounting to $876 million incurred in the third quarter of 2023.
